text: Genesis chapter number 2 verses 1 through 3 will be our text of scripture for this evening. I mentioned this morning that we finally finished Genesis chapter number 1, and it means that we have 49 more chapters to go. Praise the Lord, we're making progress, amen? Jesus is coming again, and he's coming soon. He may come before we finish the book of Genesis, amen? Genesis 2 verses 1 through 3, the Bible says this, thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and all the host of them, and on the seventh day God ended his work which he had made, and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had made. Verse 3 says God blessed the seventh day and sanctified it because in it he had rested from all his work which God created and made. The title for the message this evening is there from verse number 1, the heavens and the earth were finished. We have the great privilege of having in our Bibles the account of the week of creation, and what a blessing that is. There are so many in our world today that are asking these questions of where did it all come from, how did it all begin, when did life start, so on and so forth, and we have the word of God that tells us, that gives us the record of the week of creation. What a blessing that is. And as we study Genesis chapter number 1, we've said it often that the power and wisdom of God is on display. It is true, as we've mentioned many times throughout the study, according to Hebrews 11 and verse number 3, that it is imperative that we accept the record by faith. I'm currently reading a book and I've not finished it so I cannot fully endorse it because I've not finished the book, but the book is entitled, I Do Not Have Enough Faith to Be an Atheist, and you've read it before, and would you recommend it? Say it again? Yeah, he was co-authored, he is one of them, and the reality is that, again, I've not finished the book so I can't put my full stamp of approval on it because I've not finished it, but so far it's been an excellent read, and the reality is that they talk about, oh, you have faith in God. They have faith in what they believe too. Their faith is just misplaced, and by the way, it requires for them to have more faith even than, see because the object of our faith is God himself, and God has declared, here's what I've done, here's how I created, I said and it was done, he spoke and it was created, it existed, and what a blessing to have the account of God's Word, and certainly the awesome display of God's power and of God's wisdom should cause each of us as his children to stand in awe of who he is. That we study the first, many times, listen, most of us have read Genesis 1 countless times. In fact, there are times when we start the new year that maybe we go to Genesis 1 and we almost say, well, I've read this a hundred times, I already know, and we just kind of go through it, but as we kind of just slow down a little bit and allow the truth of what God is revealing to us, and to see his power and his wisdom, it results in us saying, what a wonderful God, what a powerful God, what an all-wise God he is, and the psalmist speaks of that in Psalm 33, in verse number eight, remember that on day number one, light or energy was created. On day number two, he separated it there and created the firmament. It was on day number three that it was the sea, the dry land, and the plant life, and we found that on day number four, it was the sun, the moon, and the stars, and we were at the Creation Museum this past week, as well as visiting the Noah's Ark encounter, and as we were there at the Creation Museum, we went into the planetarium, and I mean, it was absolutely amazing as we sat in that theater, if you will, and we sat back in our chairs, and we're looking at all of these things, and Holly was sitting to my right, and as we were sitting there looking at all of these stars, and they were going through all of the billions of miles, and light, I mean, just all of these numbers, and all of this stuff, and I kind of whispered over to Holly, and I repeated those words that we find in Genesis 1, in verse 16, he made the stars also. It's amazing, and when we were sitting there in that planetarium, and just taking it all in, and saying, this is just captured in a few words in Genesis' account, in Genesis 1 and 16, and he made the stars also. It was on day four that he made the stars, the sun, the moon, and the stars. On day number five, the sea life, and the fowls. We find that on day number six, the land-based animals, and then the crowning moment of creation when he created man in his own image, and what a blessing as we've studied through, and we've had the opportunity to read, and to see all that God gives us. We are reminded that these are six literal 24-hour days. These are not periods of time, these are not long ages of creation, these are literal 24-hour days. We understand this, really, based upon the fact that the word and is used, the usage of that word and, and giving that sequential idea of the events that are recorded. But we also understand that at the end of each day, the Bible says the evening and the morning were the second day, the third day, the fourth day, and we find that God gives us there in that reading of Genesis chapter number one, the truth concerning these six literal 24-hour days. As we come to Genesis chapter number two in verses one through three, we find that he says, thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and notice this, and all the host of them. If you're taking notes this evening, notice with me first of all that God declares his creative work complete. He says, thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and all the host of them. It tells us in verse number three, and God blessed the seventh day and sanctified it because that in it, he had rested from all his work which God created and made. And so we find that he declares his creative work complete. He says it is finished. It is complete, not continuing, and that's important for us to understand as we look at the Genesis account of creation, and we understand that God in his infinite wisdom, he chose to create everything in six days. Now, people argue about this all the time, and they have these circular reasoning arguments of, well, couldn't he have done it in one day? Sure, he's God, right? He could do whatever he chose to do. He could have created everything in a moment. He could have taken years. He could have done whatever he wanted to do, but what he did in his infinite wisdom is he created everything in six days, and that's God's right to do it that way, by the way, and he's God, amen? And he told us exactly what he did. And the real danger as we get into some of those arguments and some of those discussions and that circular reasoning of what God can or cannot do, the problem is that we must go with what God told us that he did, because we can sit around and we can say, well, yeah, God could have done that, but he told us he did this, and so therefore we must take God at his word. That is the definition of faith, by the way, that we take God at his word. We find that he created, as we've spoken about this many times throughout our study of Genesis chapter number one, that God created each kind with the ability to reproduce, but we also found that during the time of creation, and we discussed this on a number of occasions, that the genetic code within the DNA allows for that wide variation horizontally within the kind, but not for vertical evolution from one kind to another kind. And here's the interesting thing. When we think about that genetic code within the DNA and we think about the variations horizontally, if you will, within the kind, we find that that is what allows for animals to adapt to certain climates, as they would be moved from one climate to another climate as some animals are transported from one area to another area. You think about Solomon and all of his wealth and all of his wisdom and all of those things. He brought in all kinds of animals and beings and what have you, and when we think about the DNA code or the genetic code within the DNA and God creating those animals with that ability to adapt, not to evolve, but to adapt, and it's built within the genetic code within the DNA.
